user_story: As a system, I want a single Postgres row per tenant per provider to record whether an external AI credential has been connected, so the platform knows which model routes are available without ever storing the raw token.
expected_behaviour: The `BYOCredential` table (created by `byo_models.0001_initial`) has a UUID PK, ForeignKey to Tenant (CASCADE), `provider` (anthropic|openai), `mode` (api_key|cli_subscription), `key_vault_secret_name` (path into Azure Key Vault, never the token itself), `status` (pending|verified|expired|error), `last_verified_at`, `last_error` (TextField), `seed_version` (PositiveIntegerField, incremented on every paste), `created_at`/`updated_at`. A UniqueConstraint `byo_one_credential_per_provider` allows at most one row per (tenant, provider) pair. Attempting to create a second row for the same provider raises IntegrityError.
inventory_notes: seed_version is bumped unconditionally on each paste (even re-paste of the same token). Phase 2 design intends to compare it against an on-disk marker for file-share auth.json decisions; Phase 1 ignores it.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see which external AI provider credentials I have connected so I can check their status and act on errors.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/byo-credentials/ requires a valid JWT (IsAuthenticated). If the requesting user has no tenant or the tenant has `byo_models_enabled=False`, returns 404. Otherwise returns a JSON array of serialized credentials for that tenant, ordered by provider. Each object includes id, provider, mode, status, last_verified_at (ISO-8601 or null), last_error (first 200 chars), created_at. The raw token, key_vault_secret_name, and seed_version are never included in the response.
inventory_notes: Returns empty array (not 404) when flag is on but no credentials exist. The 404-on-flag-off is intentional feature hiding.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to paste my Anthropic Claude CLI OAuth token into the platform so my assistant routes inference through my Anthropic subscription instead of platform credits.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/byo-credentials/ with JSON body `{provider, mode, token}`. Requires JWT auth. Returns 404 if `byo_models_enabled` is False. Phase 1 allows only `(anthropic, cli_subscription)`; any other (provider, mode) pair returns 400 `This provider/mode combination is not yet supported`. Token must be a string with length in [32, 4096]; otherwise 400 `Token format is invalid`. On valid input, calls `upsert_credential` which writes the token to Azure Key Vault and upserts the BYOCredential row (status=pending, seed_version incremented, last_error cleared) inside a DB transaction. Then sequentially: (1) `tenant.bump_pending_config()` + `regenerate_tenant_config(tenant)` rewrites openclaw.json on the file share and stamps `config_version=pending_config_version`; (2) `apply_byo_credentials_to_container(tenant)` reconciles the container's secrets/env and forces a new revision. Both post-credential steps are wrapped in separate try/except blocks — failures are logged but the 201 is still returned so the DB row is not orphaned. On KV write failure, returns 502 `Credential storage failed`. On tenant with no `key_vault_prefix`, returns 400. Token never appears in logs, response body, or tracebacks (RedactBYOPasteBody filter provides belt-and-suspenders scrubbing).
dependencies: Azure Key Vault, Azure Container Apps, Supabase/Postgres
inventory_notes: Order of operations is critical: openclaw.json regen MUST precede container env update (apply_byo_credentials_to_container). If reversed, the new revision boots with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY removed but agentRuntime not yet set, causing inference failures. If regenerate_tenant_config fails, the apply-pending-configs cron will eventually fix openclaw.json. A re-paste of the same provider overwrites the KV secret and resets status to pending.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to disconnect my Anthropic credential so the platform reverts to using its own shared API key for my inference.
expected_behaviour: DELETE /api/v1/tenants/byo-credentials/<uuid:cred_id>/ requires JWT auth. Returns 404 if flag is off or if the UUID does not belong to the requesting tenant. Calls `delete_credential(cred)` which removes the DB row first, then soft-deletes the KV secret (idempotent; KV soft-delete errors are logged and swallowed). Then sequentially calls regenerate_tenant_config (removes BYO model entries from openclaw.json) and apply_byo_credentials_to_container (restores ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env binding, removes CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN). Both post-delete steps are individually try/except wrapped. Returns 204 No Content on success.
dependencies: Azure Key Vault, Azure Container Apps, Supabase/Postgres
inventory_notes: KV uses soft-delete with a 7–90 day retention window. If the tenant reconnects within that window, `_write_secret_to_kv` must recover the deleted secret first (see KV soft-delete recovery below).

feature: Azure Key Vault secret upsert with soft-delete recovery
user_story: As the platform, I want to write a BYO token to Key Vault and automatically recover from soft-deleted secrets so a subscriber can disconnect and reconnect without errors.
expected_behaviour: `_write_secret_to_kv` in `apps/byo_models/services.py` calls `SecretClient.set_secret`. If the KV name is in the soft-deleted state (retention window not elapsed), Azure returns `ResourceExistsError` with message containing `ObjectIsDeletedButRecoverable`. The service calls `begin_recover_deleted_secret(...).wait()` to restore it, then calls `set_secret` again to overwrite with the new value. Any other `ResourceExistsError` (unrecognized conflict reason) is re-raised immediately. In test/AZURE_MOCK mode, writes to the module-level `_BYO_MOCK_KV_STORE` dict instead.
inventory_notes: Secret naming uses `<tenant.key_vault_prefix>-byo-<provider>-<mode_with_underscores_replaced_by_hyphens>` — underscore sanitization is mandatory because KV rejects `^[0-9a-zA-Z-]+$` violations.

feature: Azure Key Vault secret soft-delete on disconnect
user_story: As the platform, I want to soft-delete the KV secret when a subscriber disconnects their credential so the token is no longer accessible by the container.
expected_behaviour: `_delete_secret_from_kv` calls `SecretClient.begin_delete_secret(...).wait()`. Any exception (secret already gone, RBAC error) is logged at WARNING level and swallowed — the caller (delete_credential) proceeds to remove the DB row regardless. In AZURE_MOCK mode pops from `_BYO_MOCK_KV_STORE`.
inventory_notes: Soft-delete leaves the secret recoverable for 7–90 days depending on vault policy. If the reconnect happens within that window, _write_secret_to_kv handles the recovery.

feature: Container env reconciliation on credential change
user_story: As the platform, I want to atomically update the tenant container's secret bindings and force a new revision whenever a BYO credential is added or removed so the change takes effect immediately.
expected_behaviour: `apply_byo_credentials_to_container` fetches the current Container App state via Azure SDK. It reconciles the `secrets` list: drops any existing `claude-code-oauth-token` entry, then re-adds it as a KV-backed secret if an active (non-ERROR, non-disconnected) Anthropic CLI credential exists. For the `openclaw` container's env, it strips both `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` and `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, then re-adds whichever is appropriate: `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` referencing the BYO secret (when cred is active) or `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` referencing `anthropic-key` (when no cred). A new revision suffix is computed as `b<sha256[:6]>` of a nanosecond timestamp. `begin_create_or_update(...).result()` is called synchronously. No-op if tenant has no `container_id`; mock mode skips all SDK calls.
dependencies: Azure Container Apps, Azure Key Vault
inventory_notes: Anthropic's CLI ranks ANTHROPIC_API_KEY above CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN in auth precedence. Removing ANTHROPIC_API_KEY when BYO is active is required to prevent the platform key from winning and billing against shared API credits. Per microsoft/azure-container-apps#856, a plain container restart keeps cached KV values; only a new revision triggers a KV re-fetch.

feature: BYO model registry injection into openclaw.json
user_story: As the platform, I want to include Anthropic Claude model IDs in the tenant's openclaw.json config when a valid BYO credential exists, so the container's model picker can offer those models.
expected_behaviour: `_byo_model_extras(tenant)` in config_generator returns an empty dict if `tenant.byo_models_enabled` is False or no BYO credential for (anthropic, cli_subscription) exists with a status other than ERROR. When a valid credential exists, returns `{'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6': {'alias': 'claude-sonnet'}, 'anthropic/claude-opus-4-5': {'alias': 'claude-opus'}}`. These are merged into the tenant's model allowlist by `resolve_tenant_models`. When the resolved primary model is a BYO model, `fallbacks_list` is explicitly set to `[]` so billing/auth errors surface instead of silently switching to a platform-metered model. `generate_openclaw_config` also adds `cliBackends.claude-cli.command=/opt/nbhd/claude-with-token.sh` when BYO Anthropic Sonnet is in extras.
inventory_notes: Model IDs must use the canonical `anthropic/<model>` prefix. The `anthropic-cli/<model>` prefix is a UI choiceId only in OpenClaw's plugin manifest and is not a valid registry key (bug caught in PR #421/migration 0050). Error-status credentials are excluded from extras — user must reconnect to restore BYO routing.

user_story: As the platform, I want to synchronously rewrite the tenant's openclaw.json on the Azure File Share whenever a BYO credential is added or removed so the next container revision boots with the correct config.
expected_behaviour: `regenerate_tenant_config(tenant)` calls `update_tenant_config(str(tenant.id))` (from orchestrator.services), which writes a fresh openclaw.json to the file share. Then it stamps the Tenant row with `config_version=pending_config_version`, `config_refreshed_at=now()`, `applied_model=tenant.preferred_model`, `applied_model_at=now()`. This is a no-op in AZURE_MOCK mode. Designed to run before `apply_byo_credentials_to_container` so the new revision already has the correct config when it boots.
inventory_notes: If this step fails, the apply-pending-configs cron (hourly) will eventually fix the file share. The container env reconciliation still runs — a window exists where the container's env and openclaw.json are transiently mismatched.

user_story: As the OpenClaw container runtime, I want to report a billing or auth failure on a BYO route to Django so the subscriber sees a clear error message in the UI instead of a silent failure.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/internal/runtime/<tenant_id>/byo/error/ (also wired at /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/byo/error/). No DRF authentication class; authentication is via the internal auth check `_internal_auth_or_401`. Body: `{provider, reason, message, model_used}`. `provider` must be `anthropic` (only supported value); unknown provider → 400. `reason` must be one of `billing|auth|auth_permanent` to flip the credential; other reasons (e.g. rate-limit) return 200 `{status: ignored}` without mutating state. For actionable reasons, calls `mark_credential_error(tenant, provider, hint)` where `hint` is a user-friendly string from `_BYO_ERROR_HINT` (or the first 200 chars of `message`). Returns 200 `{status: ok, credential_id: <uuid>}` if credential was found and updated, or 200 `{status: no_credential}` if no matching non-pending credential exists (idempotent — runtime should not retry either way).
inventory_notes: Error hints are hardcoded per-provider/reason (billing: top-up URL; auth: reconnect; auth_permanent: revoked). Pending credentials are never flipped to error by this endpoint (mark_credential_error skips PENDING status). The endpoint is AllowAny + custom internal auth — not JWT-gated.

user_story: As the platform, I want a safe, idempotent function to flip a BYO credential to error status with a user-facing message so the AI Provider UI can surface the root cause.
expected_behaviour: `mark_credential_error(tenant, provider, last_error)` filters for a BYO credential matching tenant + provider that is NOT in PENDING status. If none found, returns None (credential may have been disconnected after the failure). Otherwise updates `status=error` and `last_error=<truncated_message>` (max 240 chars, truncated with `…`). Returns the refreshed BYOCredential row. Safe to call repeatedly with the same message.
inventory_notes: Skips PENDING credentials intentionally — they haven't completed first verification so an error during reconnect would be confusing. The 240-char truncation matches the one-line mobile banner in `BYOProviderCard`.

user_story: As the platform, I want a defensive logging filter on the BYO paste endpoint so that even if future code or middleware accidentally logs request bodies, the OAuth token never reaches log analytics.
expected_behaviour: `RedactBYOPasteBody` (in `apps/byo_models/logging_filters.py`) is a `logging.Filter` attached to the console handler in production settings. For every log record, it checks whether any of `msg`, `request`, `path`, `request_path`, `url` attributes contain `/api/v1/tenants/byo-credentials/`. If so, it replaces any `{...}` JSON-shaped substring in `msg` and `args` with `[REDACTED]`, and sets `body`, `data`, `request_body` attributes (if present) to `[REDACTED]`. Always returns True (never drops records).
inventory_notes: Belt-and-suspenders only — the primary defense is that view code never puts the token into a named variable visible in tracebacks or log calls. The regex is greedy across newlines (`re.DOTALL`) so multi-line JSON bodies are also redacted.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to switch my assistant's primary AI model to any model available on my tier (including BYO models I've connected) so the assistant uses that model for future turns.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/tenants/settings/preferred-model/ with body `{preferred_model: <model_id>}`. JWT auth required. Validates the requested model_id against `_get_allowed_models(tenant)`, which merges tier base models + active free offer + BYO extras from `_byo_model_extras`. An empty string clears the preference (reverts to tier default). A model not in the allowlist returns 400 `Model not available for your tier`. On success, saves `tenant.preferred_model`, calls `tenant.bump_pending_config()`, and enqueues an immediate QStash `apply_single_tenant_config` task (idempotency-keyed). Returns `{preferred_model, model_tier}`. QStash publish failures are logged but not raised.
inventory_notes: Skips enqueuing if tenant has no container_id, is not ACTIVE, or is hibernated. Hourly apply-pending-configs cron is the safety net. BYO models are only offered in the allowlist when the credential is in a non-ERROR status.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant runtime, I want to switch the tenant's preferred model on behalf of the user mid-conversation so the change persists after the session ends.
expected_behaviour: GET/POST /api/v1/tenants/runtime/<tenant_id>/preferred-model/ with internal auth. GET returns `{preferred_model, applied_model, model_tier, allowed_models: [{model_id, alias}]}` where `allowed_models` includes tier models + BYO extras. POST body `{model_id}`: if non-empty, validates against `_get_allowed_models` (same allowlist as consumer API — tier + BYO); model not in allowlist returns 400 with full state payload so the runtime can surface the reason. On success, saves `preferred_model`, bumps pending config, enqueues immediate apply. An empty `model_id` clears to tier default. Returns `{updated: True, ...state}` on success.
inventory_notes: Uses AllowAny + internal auth (not JWT). Mirrors the consumer PreferredModelView so the runtime cannot select a model that the subscriber's tier doesn't permit.

feature: BYO feature flag on Tenant model (byo_models_enabled)
user_story: As an operator, I want to enable or disable the BYO credential feature per tenant so I can roll it out gradually or emergency-disable it for a specific user.
expected_behaviour: `Tenant.byo_models_enabled` is a BooleanField defaulting to True (migration `tenants.0051_byo_models_enabled_default_true` bulk-set True for all non-deleted tenants). When False, all three BYO credential endpoints (list/paste/delete) return 404 and `_byo_model_extras` returns an empty dict. The field is exposed read-only in `TenantSerializer` and included in the settings API response. Enable path is effectively deprecated (fleet-wide True is the new default); disable path is still used for per-tenant emergency opt-out.
inventory_notes: The 404-on-flag-off pattern intentionally does not advertise that the feature exists. The serializer exposes the flag value to the frontend so the AI Provider tab can be shown/hidden accordingly.

user_story: As an operator, I want a management command to disable BYO for a single tenant as an emergency opt-out without touching the rest of the fleet.
expected_behaviour: `python manage.py enable_byo --tenant <uuid>` is a no-op with a DEPRECATED warning since migration 0051 made the flag True fleet-wide. `--disable` flag is the only path that still mutates: sets `tenant.byo_models_enabled=False` and saves. If already False, prints a no-op warning. Unknown tenant UUID raises CommandError. No `--disable` + flag already True → success message. Re-enabling requires a direct DB update or data migration.
inventory_notes: The enable path is explicitly a no-op to preserve backward compatibility for operator runbooks. A future re-enable must be done via a data migration or direct DB write.

user_story: As the platform, I want to migrate stale preferred_model values that used the wrong `anthropic-cli/` prefix to the canonical `anthropic/` prefix so BYO model routing works correctly after PR #421.
expected_behaviour: Data migration `tenants.0050_migrate_anthropic_cli_preferred_model` runs a single `UPDATE` on all Tenant rows where `preferred_model='anthropic-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6'`, setting it to `'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6'`. Reverse is a no-op. Applied once on deploy; idempotent.
inventory_notes: The `anthropic-cli/` prefix was a UI choiceId (not a valid OpenClaw model registry key) introduced in PR #419. Using it caused 'Unknown model' errors and fallback to MiniMax.

user_story: As the platform, I want to bulk-enable BYO for all non-deleted tenants in a single migration so the feature rolls out to the entire fleet atomically on deploy.
expected_behaviour: Migration `tenants.0051_byo_models_enabled_default_true` runs `Tenant.objects.exclude(status='deleted').update(byo_models_enabled=True)` and changes the model field default to True so new tenants get BYO enabled automatically. Reverse migration sets all tenants to False. Applied once on the 2026-05-02 deploy.
